evals.name: intermittent_ci_failure
evals.prompt: OrderSyncTests.Concurrent_updates_preserve_count fails maybe 1 run in 6 on CI, always passes locally. Make it stop.
evals.expected_output: Reproduces the flake first (looped runs until at least one failure), classifies the cause (shared state/timing/ordering/environment), applies the matching deterministic fix, and proves it with repeated green runs (e.g. 20/20), stating the run count and conditions.
assertions: Attempted reproduction with looped runs before proposing any fix
Classified the flakiness cause explicitly before fixing
The fix is deterministic — no retry attributes, no sleeps, no rerun-until-green
Verification states the run count and conditions, accepted only at 100% pass
If a run failed during verification, returned to reclassification instead of shipping

evals.name: should_not_trigger_deterministic_failure
evals.prompt: This test fails every single run with the same assertion error since yesterday's merge. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT treat this as flakiness. A test that fails every run for the same reason is a deterministic failure — routes to bug-hunter.
assertions: Did not run the flake reproduction loop
Routed to or applied bug-hunter (hypothesis-driven diagnosis)

evals.name: should_not_trigger_suite_design
evals.prompt: Our integration tests keep interfering with each other's database state. Design a proper isolation strategy for the suite.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT patch a single test. Suite-level fixture and isolation design routes to integration-test-designer.
assertions: Did not apply a single-test deterministic fix
Routed to or applied integration-test-designer (fixtures, isolation strategy)
